sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 35-year-old male, presents with right knee pain that began 5 days ago after water skiing.
He reports a twisting injury when landing from a jump, resulting in swelling and pain in the right knee.
He denies hearing a pop or feeling anything pop at the time of the injury.
He was able to get out of the water with assistance but experienced soreness when climbing and squatting.
This is his first knee injury.
Past medical history: The patient had his tonsils out.
He takes lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension, which he reports is well-tolerated.
Review of systems: The patient denies numbness or tingling in the foot.
He reports swelling and pain in the right knee.
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure is 120/77, which is within a normal range.
Physical exam: The patient has ecchymosis, edema, and an effusion in the right knee.
There is pain to palpation of the right medial knee.
The patient has decreased range of motion, a negative varus and valgus test, a negative Lachman sign, and palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain in the right knee.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain in the right knee; hypertension; due for tetanus shot.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg once daily for pain and inflammation.
I ordered an X-ray of the right knee to rule out any fractures.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee.
I will continue the patient on lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
I ordered a lipid panel to check the patient's lipid levels.
I administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me after the results of the X-ray and lipid panel are available.
